rationale: Both correctly diagnose the run-level/dummy-weight issue and land on the thread's 'invalid' resolution, with similar code-path reasoning. X grounds more tightly to the actual thread by explicitly citing PR #4354 (which akshatvishu's comment referenced) and offers denser, more specific code citations (run_args.py, stage_config.py, runtime.py, assertions.py) plus practical run requirements (weights, video path, GPU mem). Y omits the #4354 reference and pads its 'Thread grounding' section with placeholder/truncated comment URLs that look like citations but don't actually resolve, slightly undercutting its grounding credibility.
